# httpdns

> DNS TXT record management server for ACME DNS-01 challenges, compatible with lego httpreq provider.

Source: https://github.com/zzci/httpdns
Online service: https://dnsall.com

## What this project does

httpdns is a self-hosted DNS server + HTTP API that handles ACME DNS-01 challenges via CNAME delegation. Users register, add their domains, set a CNAME record, then use lego/Traefik to automatically obtain SSL certificates (including wildcards).

## Architecture

Go backend with embedded React SPA frontend. Single binary deployment.

```
main.go                    Entry point, config loading, server startup
pkg/httpdns/               Core types, interfaces, config, CNAME mapping
pkg/api/                   HTTP API (JWT auth, Basic Auth, admin, present/cleanup)
pkg/database/              SQLite/PostgreSQL with schema migrations (currently v4)
pkg/nameserver/            DNS server (miekg/dns), serves TXT records from DB
web/                       React + Vite + TypeScript SPA (embedded via go:embed)
```

## Key design decisions

### CNAME delegation with deterministic nanoid subdomains
- Users add domain `example.com`, system generates subdomain `sha256(username:domain)[:8]` (e.g. `182821e6`)
- CNAME target: `_acme-challenge.example.com ‚Üí 182821e6.s.dnsall.com`
- Deterministic: same user+domain always produces same subdomain, survives DB loss
- Hash collision handled by incrementing salt and retrying (max 10 attempts)

### Three authentication layers
1. **JWT Bearer** ‚Äî Web dashboard (`/api/*` endpoints), 72h expiry
2. **HTTP Basic Auth** ‚Äî lego httpreq (`/present`, `/cleanup`), uses `username:api_key` (NOT password)
3. **X-Admin-Key header** ‚Äî Admin API (`/admin/*`), configured in config file

### FQDN resolution in present/cleanup
lego may follow CNAME and send the resolved FQDN instead of the original. `resolveSubdomain()` in `present.go` handles both:
1. Try extracting nanoid from base domain suffix (CNAME-resolved case)
2. Fall back to original domain lookup (normal case)

## Database schema (v4)

```sql
acmedns          -- key-value metadata (db_version)
users            -- id, username, password_hash, api_key, created_at
user_domains     -- user_id, domain, subdomain (nanoid), UNIQUE(user_id, domain)
txt              -- Domain, Value, LastUpdate (TXT records for DNS)
```

## Config file (TOML)

Default path: `./data/config.cfg` (relative to working directory)

Key fields:
- `general.domain` ‚Äî DNS base domain for nanoid subdomains (e.g. `s.dnsall.com`)
- `api.api_domain` ‚Äî API endpoint domain for display (e.g. `api.dnsall.com`), defaults to general.domain
- `api.jwt_secret` ‚Äî JWT signing key (auto-generated if empty, set for persistence)
- `api.admin_key` ‚Äî Admin API key (empty = disabled)
- `database.engine` ‚Äî `sqlite` or `postgres`
- `database.connection` ‚Äî file path for SQLite, connection string for PostgreSQL

## API endpoints

### httpreq (Basic Auth: username + api_key)
- `POST /present` ‚Äî `{fqdn, value}` store TXT record
- `POST /cleanup` ‚Äî `{fqdn, value}` remove TXT record

### Public
- `POST /api/register` ‚Äî `{username, password}` ‚Üí `{token, username, api_key}`
- `POST /api/login` ‚Äî `{username, password}` ‚Üí `{token, username, api_key}`
- `GET /api/info` ‚Äî `{base_domain, api_domain}`

### User (JWT Bearer)
- `GET /api/profile` ‚Äî `{username, api_key}`
- `POST /api/profile/regenerate-key` ‚Äî `{api_key}`
- `GET /api/domains` ‚Äî `[{domain, subdomain, cname_target}]`
- `POST /api/domains` ‚Äî `{domain}` ‚Üí `{domain, subdomain, cname_target}`
- `DELETE /api/domains/:domain`
- `GET /api/records` ‚Äî `[{domain, value, last_update}]`

### Admin (X-Admin-Key header)
- `GET /admin/users`, `POST /admin/users`, `DELETE /admin/users/:id`
- `GET /admin/domains`, `POST /admin/domains`, `DELETE /admin/domains/:domain`
- `GET /admin/records`

## Usage with lego

```bash
LEGO_DISABLE_CNAME_SUPPORT=true \
HTTPREQ_ENDPOINT=https://api.dnsall.com \
HTTPREQ_USERNAME=myuser \
HTTPREQ_PASSWORD=<api_key> \
lego --dns httpreq --dns.propagation-disable-ans \
  --domains example.com --domains "*.example.com" \
  --email admin@example.com --accept-tos run
```

Key flags:
- `LEGO_DISABLE_CNAME_SUPPORT=true` ‚Äî prevent lego from rewriting FQDN after CNAME resolution
- `--dns.propagation-disable-ans` ‚Äî skip authoritative NS propagation check (records are on httpdns, not the domain's NS)

## Traefik integration

```yaml
certificatesResolvers:
  letsencrypt:
    acme:
      dnsChallenge:
        provider: httpreq
        propagation:
          disableChecks: true
```

Environment: `LEGO_DISABLE_CNAME_SUPPORT=true`, `HTTPREQ_ENDPOINT`, `HTTPREQ_USERNAME`, `HTTPREQ_PASSWORD`

## Build

```bash
cd web && npm ci && npx vite build && cd ..
CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -o httpdns .
```

Docker: multi-stage (node frontend ‚Üí go backend ‚Üí alpine runtime), cross-compiled via `--platform=$BUILDPLATFORM` + `GOOS/GOARCH`.

## Testing

```bash
go test ./pkg/...
```

All tests use in-memory SQLite (`:memory:`). The `TestFileCheckPermissionDenied` test has been removed.

## Dependencies

- `miekg/dns` ‚Äî DNS protocol
- `julienschmidt/httprouter` ‚Äî HTTP router
- `golang-jwt/jwt/v5` ‚Äî JWT tokens
- `golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt` ‚Äî password hashing
- `caddyserver/certmagic` ‚Äî auto HTTPS for the API itself
- `glebarez/go-sqlite` ‚Äî pure Go SQLite (CGO_ENABLED=0)
- `lib/pq` ‚Äî PostgreSQL driver
- `BurntSushi/toml` ‚Äî config parsing
- `go.uber.org/zap` ‚Äî structured logging
